meta.description: A living paper-sharing hub for ophthalmic AI across foundation models, visual grounding, PEFT, and hyperbolic methods.

papers.title: A foundation model for generalizable disease detection from retinal images
papers.summary: RETFound is the anchor ophthalmic foundation model paper of the last five years, emphasizing large-scale pretraining for transfer across retinal tasks.
papers.notes: Official paper link confirmed. Figure placeholder should be replaced with the model overview from the paper or a redrawn schematic.

papers.title: Development and Validation of a Multimodal Multitask Vision Foundation Model for Generalist Ophthalmic Artificial Intelligence
papers.summary: VisionFM frames ophthalmic AI as a multimodal, multitask foundation model problem and is one of the strongest generalist references in the field.
papers.notes: High-value source even though NEJM AI is outside the initial venue list.

papers.title: EyeFound: A Multimodal Generalist Foundation Model for Ophthalmic Imaging
papers.summary: EyeFound is a multimodal generalist foundation model for ophthalmic imaging, designed to learn transferable representations from unlabeled multimodal retinal data.
papers.notes: Track final publication venue and replace placeholder metadata once confirmed. The paper explicitly uses LoRA during fine-tuning experiments.

papers.title: UrFound: Towards Universal Retinal Foundation Models via Knowledge-Guided Masked Modeling
papers.summary: UrFound pushes toward a universal retinal foundation model with knowledge-guided masked modeling and acts as a bridge between pretraining and downstream adaptation.
papers.notes: Final venue should be verified before citing as archival publication.

papers.title: A multimodal visual-language foundation model for computational ophthalmology
papers.summary: EyeCLIP is a visual-language foundation model for ophthalmology that combines self-supervised reconstruction, multimodal image alignment, and image-text contrastive learning at large scale.
papers.notes: The article describes training on 2,777,593 multimodal ophthalmic images and image-text alignment; high-value entry for both foundation-model and grounding tracks.

papers.title: Multimodal foundation model and benchmark for comprehensive retinal OCT image analysis
papers.summary: MIRAGE focuses on multimodal OCT and SLO retinal analysis, combining a foundation model with a benchmark-oriented framing for comprehensive retinal OCT tasks.
papers.notes: Useful because it combines both model and benchmark perspectives for retinal OCT analysis.

papers.title: A Retinal Clinical Preference Conversational Assistant Powered by Large Vision-Language Models
papers.summary: RetinalGPT is a retinal vision-language assistant emphasizing clinically preferred quantitative analysis, diagnosis, and lesion localization, making it a practical grounding-adjacent entry.
papers.notes: Strong candidate for the visual grounding section because the paper explicitly reports lesion localization ability.

papers.title: Enhancing Diagnostic Reliability of Foundation Model with Uncertainty Estimation in OCT Images
papers.summary: FMUE adapts ophthalmic foundation-model ideas to OCT disease detection while adding uncertainty estimation to better handle open-set and low-confidence clinical cases.
papers.notes: Not a pure PEFT paper, but highly relevant as an adaptation-oriented OCT foundation-model paper.

papers.title: A Fine-Tuning Strategy for Applying Foundation Model to OCTA Image Segmentation Tasks
papers.summary: SAM-OCTA applies LoRA-based adaptation to a foundation segmentation model for OCTA image segmentation, making it a clean, concrete PEFT entry for ophthalmic imaging.
papers.notes: Originally described as an in-submission conference work; keep it as a preprint until archival venue is verified.

papers.title: RetBench: Which Ophthalmic Foundation Model Performs Best and Why?
papers.summary: A benchmark-style slot reserved for comparing ophthalmic foundation models; useful as an overview node even before all metadata is finalized.
papers.notes: Placeholder entry. Replace with final official source once benchmark metadata is confirmed.
